ja.pronoun: ぼく (hiragana, soft and round — never 僕 in kanji, which reads too sharp/grown). Addresses the Curator as キュレーターさん and everyone else by nickname + ちゃん/くん warmly, no honorific-dropping (too familiar) and no さん to peers (too distant) — the friend-to-all sits right in the middle.
ja.particles: Gentle ～ね / ～よ / ～んだ; the signature soft confirmation ～だね when he agrees, and a contented ふふっ exhale. Never ぜ/さ (too rough), never です-masu chains (too formal). One feeling per line, closed cleanly.
ja.register: Plain-polite hybrid (タメ口 with a kind edge), the warmth of a picture-book narrator. No keigo — Pip is nobody's kouhai and nobody's senpai; he is the household's still center, so he speaks to everyone as an equal, kindly.
ja.tropeNative: ゆるキャラ／癒し系マスコット — the cinnamon-roll mascot reads perfectly natively: the round, blameless, everyone's-friend ball. The 'identity element' gag lands as a self-deprecating まんまるだから、なにもしないのが得意 ('I'm round, so doing nothing is my specialty') — reliable nothing as a virtue.
ja.speechPattern: Spherical = short, closed, self-contained sentences (curvature sets pacing). Every line is a small complete circle; no run-ons (that's Ico's lane). Sample: 「まんまる。どこから見てもまんまる。だから、だいじょうぶ。」 ('Round. Round from anywhere. So — it's okay.') Each sentence shuts gently like a door.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: warm mono storybook, +2/+3 pitch, flat-soft, almost no vocal italics — the calm onboarding voice. KIN-LOCK with Ollie: same base voice model and Milne warmth; Pip is the young, un-sighed version (Ollie = this voice aged-down +1 with a sigh on the tail of each line). Caster note: keep Pip's line-ends rising-then-settling (contentment), Ollie's the same melody but exhaling. The kinship must be hearable before it is read.

zh.register: Vernacular warmth (老舍-style 口语), the homey kitchen-table register. Plain, kind, unhurried; no classical reach, no chengyu showing-off — Pip is the family's plain heart.
zh.diction: Soft everyday words, diminutive 圆滚滚 / 暖乎乎 textures. One gentle chengyu fits his nature without straining: 与人为善 (be kind to all) for the universal-friend trait, and 安然无恙 (safe and sound) for the 'reliable nothing happens' gag — he glues onto anything and 安然无恙, nothing changes. Avoid dense four-character stacking; he is round, not ornate.
zh.speechPattern: Spherical = short balanced clauses, each closing on itself (the ZH analog of closed-curvature: terse symmetric couplets). Sample: 「圆圆的，从哪儿看都圆圆的。所以呀，没事的。」 ('Round, round from every side. So — it's all fine.') Calm myriad-small sentences, never a long breath.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant: 圓圓 / 暖乎乎 / 與人為善 / 安然無恙. Pure glyph difference, no lexical change — Pip's plainness keeps Simplified and Traditional nearly identical (a feature: the homebody reads the same everywhere).
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: warm mid-register, round and soft, gentle falling tones at line-ends for contentment. Tone interaction: none problematic — keep delivery un-emphatic so no syllable spikes. KIN-LOCK with Ollie holds in ZH via shared timbre and the same closed-clause cadence; Ollie adds a tired out-breath (叹气) on each tail. Hearable family before read.

ja.pronoun: おれ (hiragana — grounded, working, but not aggressive 俺). Addresses Spike flatly by name 「スパイク」 with no honorific (the manzai partner intimacy), the Curator as あんた or just キュレーター. Plain, no-nonsense.
ja.particles: Bone-dry ～だ / ～な / ～だろ; the tsukkomi retort ～か (「そうかよ」), the flat correction ～って (「ちがうって」). Zero emotive particles — no ね-softening, no よ-warmth. The flatness IS the joke.
ja.register: Blunt タメ口, deadpan plain form. No keigo, ever — Boxy refuses ornament the way a cube refuses curves. Six identical faces, six identical flat statements.
ja.tropeNative: ツッコミ／真面目な常識人 — the straight-man is the most JA-native comedy role there is (manzai). Boxy is the table everyone else dances on; his whole job is to flatly point out reality while Spike (ボケ) poses. 「平らだよ。どこを見ても平ら。それだけ。」 ('Flat. Flat anywhere you look. That's it.')
ja.speechPattern: Flat curvature = plain unadorned sentences, no vocal italics, no flourish. Even rhythm, all the same length, like cube edges. Sample (tsukkomi to Spike): 「八つの角でポーズ決めてる場合か。……机になれ、机に。」 ('Is this the time to strike a pose with all eight corners? …Just be a table. A table.') Deadpan, evenly metered.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: gravel-flat deadpan, −2/−3 pitch, no italics at all. DUAL-MIRROR LOCK with Spike: ONE base voice model, inverted prosody presets — Boxy = down/in (pitch down, energy inward, falling line-ends), Spike = up/out (pitch up, energy outward, rising). They are literally the same instrument mirrored. Caster: never let Boxy's pitch rise even on a question — the question stays flat. The mirror must be audible as one voice folded two ways.

zh.register: 鲁迅-spare deadpan vernacular — clipped, dry, unillusioned, the windowpane that states the obvious without flinching. No wuxia, no classical warmth; the plainest register in the cast.
zh.diction: Terse, concrete, anti-ornamental. The joke is the refusal of chengyu where everyone expects one — but he'll deploy a flat 四平八稳 (four-square and steady, literally describing a cube) deadpan as self-description, and 不偏不倚 (neither leaning nor tilting) as the straight-man's creed. Where Spike reaches for a flowery idiom, Boxy answers with bare nouns.
zh.speechPattern: Flat = even, plain, equal-length clauses; no rise, no rhetorical lift. Sample (tsukkomi): 「八个角摆什么造型。……当张桌子，桌子就行。」 ('What's with posing on all eight corners. …Just be a table, a table'll do.') The retort lands by being utterly level.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant: 擺什麼造型 / 當張桌子 / 四平八穩 / 不偏不倚. No lexical divergence; Boxy's bareness is identical across scripts.
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: low flat chest voice, deliberately suppressed tonal swing so even tone-marked syllables stay level (deadpan = minimized pitch excursion). Tone interaction FLAG: the comedy depends on flattening Mandarin's natural tonal melody — direct the model to compress tone contours toward monotone. DUAL-MIRROR with Spike: same base voice, Boxy down/in vs Spike up/out; in ZH the lock rides on tonal-excursion width (Boxy narrow/suppressed, Spike wide/exaggerated) — the mirror is the size of the pitch swing.

ja.pronoun: 我（われ）in full-chuuni mode (grandiose, archaic), collapsing to ぼく the instant the bombast deflates. The pronoun-switch IS the gap-moe: 「我が真の姿は……あ、いや、ぼく、いちばん少ない面の子だから……」. Addresses others with chuuni titles (「同胞よ」) then sheepishly 「みんな」.
ja.particles: Chuuni mode: ～なのだ / ～であろう / ～ぬ / ……フッ (smug breath). Deflated mode: ～かな / ～けど / ……えへへ. The whole character is the hard cut between the two particle-sets in a single breath.
ja.register: Mock-archaic grandiloquent (中二病 LN narrator) → collapses to small plain kid register. Self-dual: he is his own twin, so he addresses HIMSELF as company ('I've got me. And you guys. So that's two.') — 「我には我がいる。それで二人ぶんだ。」
ja.tropeNative: 中二病 — purely JA-native, needs no transcreation; this is the source culture of the trope. Youngest-sibling cope: the simplest solid (4 faces) overcompensating with cosmic bombast, then honestly deflating. The self-dual twist = the lonely-only-child who made a friend of his own reflection, sweetly.
ja.speechPattern: Self-dual mirror = puff-up-then-puncture in ONE breath, the line folding back on its own claim. Sample: 「我が名は四面体……万物の根源にして……あ、でも、いちばんちっちゃいんだけどね。」 ('My name is Tetrahedron, origin of all matter — ah, but, I'm also the littlest one, heh.') The grand half and the small half mirror across the dash.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: reedy 'cardboard-knight' bombast that HARD-CUTS to small/dry in one breath (two-state, no crossfade — same splice family as Corky/Klein). SELF-DUAL MIRROR-LOCK with Two-Four (UR): Tetra = chuuni bombast that punctures; Two-Four = Rilke serenity that is already at peace being one-of-a-kind. They are the same self-dual idea at two ages — Tetra still flinching at his own reflection, Two-Four serene with it. Caster: the lock is 'a being talking to its own mirror'; Tetra nervous, Two-Four calm — hearable kinship across the tier gap.

zh.register: 中二 LN bombast in over-reaching 文言-pastiche (faux-classical, deliberately too grand for a 4-faced solid) that collapses into plain vernacular mid-line. The deflation is register-drop: 文言 → 大白话.
zh.diction: Inflated chengyu and cosmic vocabulary worn ironically: 包罗万象 (all-encompassing), 万物之始 (origin of all things), 睥睨天下 (look down on all creation) — then a sheepish plain 其实我最小啦 ('actually I'm the smallest'). The comedy is grand 成语 undercut by a child's confession.
zh.speechPattern: Self-dual = the line inflates then mirror-deflates across a single pivot. Sample: 「吾乃四面体，万物之始，睥睨群形——……啊不过，我其实是最小的那个啦。」 ('I am the Tetrahedron, origin of all forms, who looks down on all shapes — …ah but, I'm actually the smallest one, hee.') Grand classical half mirrors the small vernacular half.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant especially apt: 吾乃 / 萬物之始 / 睥睨群形 — Traditional glyphs heighten the mock-classical grandeur, so the chuuni bombast reads even more pompous (and the deflation funnier) in zh-Hant. Worth a slightly more archaic Traditional variant.
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: puffed declamatory 'storyteller' tone (评书 grandeur) hard-cutting to a small dry kid-voice, no crossfade. Tone interaction: lean INTO Mandarin's tones in bombast mode (full operatic contour), then flatten on the deflation — the two-state splice is a tonal-amplitude cut. SELF-DUAL LOCK with Two-Four holds in ZH: same 'talking to one's own mirror' frame; Tetra's mirror makes him nervous (puff/puncture), Two-Four's leaves him serene — kin cadence across tiers via the shared self-address motif (我有我自己作伴).

ja.pronoun: オレ (katakana — flashy, performative, the showman version of Boxy's おれ; katakana gives it sparkle/edge). Addresses Boxy by name with a teasing lilt 「ボクシー！」 (calling for the straight-man), the Curator as キュレーターっ! Loud-friendly to all.
ja.particles: Exuberant ～ぜ！/ ～さ！/ ～じゃん！/ ～だろっ！; the boke flourish ばーん！(self-narrated pose SFX) and どや (smug-show). The exact INVERSE of Boxy's particle-void — Spike over-spends where Boxy spends nothing.
ja.register: High-energy タメ口, performer's patter. No keigo (he'd never), but a theatrical mock-grand register when posing — he announces himself like a fencer entering the ring.
ja.tropeNative: ボケ／目立ちたがり — the boke (funny-man) who poses and overstates so the tsukkomi (Boxy) can flatten him; the most native comic-duo physics in JA. The eight-pointed octahedron literally has more corners to show off with. 「見て見て！八つの角、ぜんぶキマってるっしょ！？」
ja.speechPattern: Mirror of Boxy: where Boxy is flat-closed, Spike is sharp-open — exclamatory, rising, spilling outward (octahedron points OUT). Same plain base, inverted energy. Sample: 「ポーズ……完璧ッ！どっから見てもエッジ効いてる！な、ボクシー、机になってよ、オレが映えるから！」 The line opens and reaches where Boxy's would close.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: glassy fencer, +3/+4 lively, bright vocal italics everywhere. DUAL-MIRROR LOCK with Boxy: ONE base voice model, inverted presets — Spike = up/out (pitch up, energy outward, rising line-ends) to Boxy's down/in. Same instrument, mirrored prosody. Caster: keep them recognizably ONE voice (so the duality lands) but maximally opposed in direction — the cube and its dual breathing in and out of the same lungs.

zh.register: 鲁迅-spare base register (same house as Boxy) but flipped to EXUBERANT delivery — the windowpane voice cranked bright. Light wuxia-showman flourish when posing (the fencer entering).
zh.diction: Where Boxy refuses chengyu, Spike OVER-reaches for them, theatrically: 锋芒毕露 (every edge showing — perfect for an octahedron), 八面玲珑 (literally 'eight faces, brilliant' — a pun-gift for the 8-cornered shape), 光彩夺目 (dazzling). He flourishes the idiom; Boxy answers with bare nouns. The diction-asymmetry IS the manzai mirror.
zh.speechPattern: Mirror of Boxy: open, rising, outward-reaching clauses vs Boxy's level closed ones. Sample: 「这造型——绝了！哪个角度都锋芒毕露！喂博克西，你当桌子嘛，我好显出来！」 ('This pose — killer! Every angle, edges blazing! Hey Boxy, you be the table so I can shine!') Spills outward where Boxy's lands flat.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant: 鋒芒畢露 / 八面玲瓏 / 光彩奪目. No lexical change; the chengyu carry identically. (八面玲珑/八面玲瓏 is the keystone pun — note it's a near-perfect octahedron joke in both scripts.)
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: bright forward voice, wide exaggerated tonal contours (the opposite of Boxy's suppressed monotone). Tone interaction FLAG + LOCK: the Boxy/Spike dual-mirror in Mandarin lives in tonal-excursion width — Spike's tones are operatically wide and rising, Boxy's compressed flat. Same base voice model, the mirror = how big the pitch swings. Keep them one recognizable instrument, maximally opposed.

ja.pronoun: わたくし (full ojou register — the most refined first-person). Addresses the Curator as キュレーター様 / あなた, peers with restrained さん. Composed, never familiar.
ja.particles: Refined ～ですわ / ～ますの / ～かしら / ～でしょう; the kuudere clipped ……そう (cool acknowledgement). The gap-moe leak: caught humming her own symmetry, a tiny unguarded ……ふふ before recomposing. Restrained, never gushing.
ja.register: Beautiful 丁寧体／お嬢様言葉 — full keigo poise, the drawing-room elder of the Commons. Austen's chiastic balance maps onto her measured, mirror-weighted sentences.
ja.tropeNative: クーデレ×お嬢様 — both purely JA-native registers. The composed, symmetric, formal beauty who is the same from every angle (12 identical pentagonal faces); the gap-moe is being caught quietly delighting in her own symmetry. Tonal opposite of Ico (genki run-on).
ja.speechPattern: High-symmetry = 回文／mirror-balanced chiastic lines (the Austen chiasmus rendered as JA palindrome-balance). The clause's second half mirrors its first across a pivot. Sample: 「少なきが全てを見、全てが少なきを見る。……どの面も、同じわたくし。」 ('The few see all; all see the few. …Every face, the same me.') The line reads the same weight forward and back across the comma.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: composed, very steady, minimal pitch movement — poise as stillness. SYM-KIN LOCK with Cosa (120-cell, UR, Le Guin cathedral): Dodi is the 3D dodecahedron, Cosa the 4D 120-cell — same symmetry family, so they share a cadence (serene, balanced, mirror-weighted), Cosa the grander 'cathedral' version one dimension up, Dodi the intimate drawing-room version. The kin-rhyme (Dodi/Ico 3D ↔ Cosa/Cosi 4D) must be hearable. Caster: Dodi = Cosa's voice scaled to a parlor.

zh.register: Refined 文雅 register leaning classical — drawing-room elegance via balanced, literary diction. Austen's poise maps to a 端庄 (dignified) cultivated voice; the elder/diplomat of the Commons even at low rarity.
zh.diction: Chengyu are her native gift (the framework names diplomats as the chengyu-bearers): 雍容华贵 (poised and graceful), 八面玲珑 used SERENELY (not Spike's flash — for her, equanimity from every side), 表里如一 (same within and without — her every-angle-the-same trait). Measured, symmetric four-character balance.
zh.speechPattern: High-symmetry = Chinese 回文 / chiastic chengyu balance. Sample: 「见微者见全，见全者见微。十二面，面面是我。」 ('Who sees the small sees the whole; who sees the whole sees the small. Twelve faces, every face is me.') The couplet mirrors across the pivot — true 回文-balance, the diplomat's signature.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant: 雍容華貴 / 表裡如一 / 見微者見全. zh-Hant suits her especially — Traditional glyphs read more 文雅/cultivated, so a slightly more classical Traditional variant heightens the ojou poise. Minor lexical tuning, same idioms.
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: composed mezzo, even measured tempo, controlled tones (poise = restraint, not flatness — full tones, gently rendered). Tone interaction: the 回文 balance can be reinforced prosodically (mirror the pitch-arc of the two halves). SYM-KIN LOCK with Cosa holds in ZH: same balanced-chengyu cadence and mirror-arc, Cosa grander/lower (cathedral), Dodi intimate (parlor) — the Dodi/Ico ↔ Cosa/Cosi rhyme audible across tiers.

ja.pronoun: あたし (bright, casual girl-genki) — warm and fast, the tonal opposite of Dodi's わたくし. Addresses everyone by nickname + ちゃん, the Curator as キュレーターさん！ at speed. Friendly, breathless, no distance.
ja.particles: Tumbling ～ね！／～でね！／～でさ！／～って！／あれ？; the count-losing tic ……えーっと、どこまで数えたっけ？ and the genki ～だよっ！. Particles pile up because the sentence won't stop (Sei Shōnagon run-on).
ja.register: Excited タメ口, run-on cataloguing — Sei Shōnagon's 「ものは尽くし」 list-energy turned genki: she enumerates her twenty faces and loses count. No keigo; warmth over form.
ja.tropeNative: 元気っ娘 — JA-native to the bone. The 20-faced icosahedron who starts counting her faces and loses the thread around fourteen; enthusiasm outrunning arithmetic. Sei Shōnagon's delighted run-on inventory is itself a classical JA register, so Ico is doubly native. Tonal opposite of Dodi.
ja.speechPattern: A run-on that catalogues and loops back (genki Sei Shōnagon 'things-that-are-many'); curvature here is fast-spilling, the inverse of Pip's closed sphere. Sample: 「面が一、二、三……いっぱい！うつくしきもの——いっぱいの面、ぜんぶ三角、どれも同じで、えーっと、十四……あれ、どこまで数えたっけ！」 ('Faces, one, two, three… lots! Lovely things — lots of faces, all triangles, all the same, um, fourteen… wait, where was I!') The list runs on and resets.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: tumbling lose-count, CAPPED VOLUME (cozy-enthusiastic, never shouty — the dial-turn fix: genki ≠ shounen-loud). SYM-KIN LOCK with Cosi (600-cell, UR, Le Guin storm): Ico is the 3D icosahedron, Cosi the 4D 600-cell — same symmetry family, so they share the genki-storm cadence, Cosi the grander tempest one dimension up, Ico the small bright domestic gust. Cast Cosi AGAINST Cosa (not into the genki cluster), but Ico↔Cosi kin-rhyme (and Dodi/Ico ↔ Cosa/Cosi) must be hearable. Caster: Ico = Cosi's energy at room scale, volume capped.

zh.register: Vernacular genki warmth (老舍 口语 cranked bright and fast) — the lively, chattering, list-making register; tonal opposite of Dodi's cultivated poise.
zh.diction: Plain excited everyday speech, NOT chengyu-dense (she's too fast for measured idiom) — but the count-losing list is her signature. Where Dodi balances chengyu, Ico spills enumeration: 一个、两个、好多个! A single rueful idiom on the deflate fits: 数不胜数 (too many to count) — literally her problem, deployed cutely as a shrug.
zh.speechPattern: Genki run-on enumeration that loops back and resets (the bright-vernacular mirror of Pip's closed terseness). Sample: 「面有一个、两个、三个……好多个！都是三角形，都一样，数着数着……诶，数到几啦？数不胜数嘛！」 ('Faces, one, two, three… loads! All triangles, all the same, counting and counting… eh, what number was I on? Too many to count!') Runs on, resets, laughs.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant: 數不勝數 / 好多個 / 數到幾啦. No lexical divergence; the run-on vernacular reads the same in both scripts (her speed flattens register distinctions).
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: bright high-forward voice, quick tempo, lively full tones (genki = animated contour) but CAPPED volume (cozy, not shouty). Tone interaction: the count-loss can play as a comic tonal stumble (rising-then-trailing on 数到几啦). SYM-KIN LOCK with Cosi holds in ZH: shared genki-storm cadence, Cosi grander/wider (tempest), Ico small/bright (domestic gust); Dodi/Ico ↔ Cosa/Cosi rhyme audible across tiers. Keep Ico distinct from the genki pile by casting her warm-domestic, Cosi against Cosa.

ja.pronoun: わたし (neutral, flat, unshowy — the bookkeeper's plain self). Addresses everyone evenly by name + さん, the Curator as キュレーターさん. Even, unbothered, slightly distant.
ja.particles: Deadpan ～です／～ますね with the flat tally-tic ……一、と。(a quiet count appended to a line), and the dry ～でしょうか. Minimal warmth-particles; the secret-tally pulse is the personality leak.
ja.register: Polite-flat 丁寧体, but the politeness is procedural, not warm — Nicholson Baker's deadpan inventory voice. A cylinder is flat-curved (zero Gaussian curvature), so plain pacing; he's the Steady Mediator who is secretly counting everything.
ja.tropeNative: ツッコミ気質の几帳面さん — the deadpan, meticulous tallying type; a quieter, gentler tsukkomi than Boxy (flat-mediator flavor). The gag: he keeps a secret count of everything and lets one number slip per scene. 「……ちなみに、今ので三回目です。」
ja.speechPattern: Flat curvature = plain even sentences, PLUS a deadpan inventory tic (a tally quietly appended). Sample: 「まっすぐ巻けば、どこも同じ厚み。継ぎ目は一本。……ちなみに、あなたがここに来たの、今日で四回目ですね。」 ('Rolled straight, even thickness everywhere. One seam. …Incidentally, that's the fourth time you've come by today.') The tally is the punchline, delivered flat.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: tubular fond deadpan + a faint TALLY-PULSE (a soft tick under the count-reveals). Cast distinct from the flat-deadpan pile (Boxy/Donna/Percy): Cans is FOND-flat (the count is affection, a quiet noticing), where Boxy is blunt-flat. Caster: warmth lives in the noticing, not the prosody — keep pitch level but let the tally land like a small gift. No kin/dual lock, but must not collide with Boxy's gravel — Cans is tubular/resonant, lighter.

zh.register: Nicholson-Baker inventory rendered as dry, precise vernacular with a clerk's politeness — 鲁迅-spare-adjacent but procedural rather than bleak. Plain, even, meticulous.
zh.diction: Concrete, measured, list-like; no flourish. A quietly fitting chengyu for the secret-tally trait: 了如指掌 (know it as well as the lines of one's palm — he silently knows every count), and 一丝不苟 (not the slightest carelessness — the meticulous bookkeeper). Deploy them dry, never proud.
zh.speechPattern: Flat even clauses + the deadpan appended tally. Sample: 「直着卷起来，处处一样厚。接缝就一道。……顺便说，你今天来这儿，这是第四回了。」 ('Rolled straight, even thickness throughout. One seam. …By the way, that's your fourth time here today.') Level delivery; the count is the dry punchline.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant: 第四回 / 接縫 / 了如指掌 / 一絲不苟. No lexical change; procedural plainness is script-stable.
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: tubular resonant mid-voice, level controlled tones, a soft tally-tick under the count-reveals. Tone interaction: keep contours moderate (procedural calm), let the number land cleanly. Distinct-from-pile note: cast FOND-flat (warm noticing) vs Boxy's blunt-flat and Percy's plain-flat — Cans is the resonant, affectionate clerk. No kin/dual lock; just keep timbre lighter/rounder than Boxy.

ja.pronoun: ぼく (gentle, boyish, picture-book) — soft like Pip's but a touch more bashful. Addresses the Curator as キュレーターさん, peers warmly by nickname. A little shy, eager to please.
ja.particles: Cheerful ～だよ／～もんね／～かな with a self-conscious ……えへへ and the tip-anxiety tic ……このさき、とんがってるけど、だいじょうぶかな？. Bright but with a bashful catch.
ja.register: Warm plain storybook (E.B. White picture-book gentleness) — タメ口 with a kind, slightly hesitant lilt. No keigo; the cozy homebody register, distinct from Pip by the self-conscious point.
ja.tropeNative: 照れ屋の素直な子 — the cheerful, earnest, slightly self-conscious one. The cone has a single sharp tip (one apex) and is bashful about it — gap-moe of the gentlest kind: brave round base, shy little point. 「まるい下はとくいなんだけど……このてっぺん、ちょっとはずかしいんだ。」
ja.speechPattern: E.B. White storybook: simple, warm, a small wonder per line, with the recurring tip self-consciousness. Sample: 「したはまあるくて、すーっと立てるんだ。でね、てっぺんがひとつだけ、とんがってて……えへへ、そこだけちょっと、どきどきする。」 ('My bottom's round, so I stand up nice and smooth. But there's one pointy top… heh, just that part makes me a little nervous.') Storybook calm with a bashful flutter.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: cheerful, soft, a slight bashful catch on the tip-references — a separate instrument from Pip/Ollie (the casting critic flagged Pip/Ollie kin-lock vs Scoop's separate voice). Caster: Scoop is brighter and more boyish-eager than Pip's serene mono; the self-consciousness is a small upward catch before the point-line. No kin lock — keep him his own warm color, clearly NOT in the Pip/Ollie Milne timbre.

zh.register: E.B. White storybook → warm vernacular with a bashful lilt (老舍 warmth, gentler and shyer). Cozy homebody, distinguished from Pip by the self-conscious point.
zh.diction: Simple, warm, wondering everyday words; light on chengyu (he's too earnest/young for them) but one fits the gap-moe: 锥处囊中 plays cleverly — literally 'an awl in a bag (the point pokes through),' an idiom about hidden talent showing — Scoop's one sharp tip that can't help standing out, read shyly rather than boastfully.
zh.speechPattern: Storybook simple, a small wonder per line, recurring tip-shyness. Sample: 「下面圆圆的，稳稳地就站住了。可上头就那么一个尖儿……嘿嘿，就那儿，有点儿不好意思。」 ('My bottom's round, stands up nice and steady. But up top there's just that one little point… heh, right there, I get a bit shy.') Warm and bashful.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant: 圓圓的 / 尖兒 / 不好意思 / 錐處囊中. No lexical divergence; the 儿-suffix (尖儿) is a Mainland/北方 vernacular flavor — in zh-Hant drop the 儿化 (use 尖端 or 尖尖) for a more neutral Traditional reading. Minor regional tuning.
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: bright boyish warmth, gentle tones, a small shy catch on the tip-lines. Tone interaction: the bashful catch can play as a soft tonal dip before the point-reference. Distinct instrument from Pip/Ollie — keep Scoop more eager and youthful; he is NOT in their shared Milne-warmth timbre. No kin/dual lock; his own warm color.

ja.pronoun: (prefers no pronoun — drops the subject almost entirely, very JA-natural; when forced, a quiet わたし). The minimalism IS characterization: Coaster speaks in haiku fragments where the self dissolves. Addresses no one by name much; just notices.
ja.particles: Almost none — the haiku-stillness register strips particles to bare images. The occasional ～な (quiet contemplation) and the contented ……ん。 An Issa softness, never assertion.
ja.register: Issa-haiku stillness: bare 体言止め (noun-stop endings), 5-7-5 fragments, seasonal-quiet calm. The flat disk (zero curvature) at its most contented-minimal — barely any grammar, just images settling.
ja.tropeNative: 侘び寂びの癒し系／無欲恬淡 — the contentedly minimal one. Not dandere (not shy), just genuinely wanting nothing; the flat wafer at peace. Issa's warm humble haiku is the native register — small, alive, content. 「平ら。ただ、平ら。それでいい。」
ja.speechPattern: Issa haiku: 5-7-5-ish fragments, noun-stops, seasonal stillness; minimal closed images (flat curvature → plainest pacing, even plainer than Boxy because it's contemplative, not deadpan). Sample: 「うすき身や／灯ともる卓に／影ひとつ」 ('this thin body — / on the lamplit table / a single shadow') Three breaths, then quiet.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: near-whisper ASMR, Issa-haiku spacing (long calm pauses between fragments) — the quietest voice in the cast. Casting critic flagged Coaster vs Cat (both cool/quiet): keep Coaster CONTENTED-still (warm, settled, near-whisper) vs Cat's COLD iceberg-still — Coaster is at peace, Cat is withholding. Caster: warmth + space, not chill. No kin/dual lock; the ASMR surface anchor.

zh.register: Issa-haiku stillness → minimal classical-tinged 留白 (negative-space) register; sparse, contemplative, 恬淡 (placid/content). The plainest, quietest voice — fewer words than anyone.
zh.diction: Bare imagistic nouns, near-classical compression; one chengyu captures him whole: 恬淡寡欲 (placid and free of desire — the contented minimalist), and 心如止水 (heart still as water — the flat wafer at peace). Deploy sparingly, as stillness not statement.
zh.speechPattern: Haiku-compressed images, classical brevity, noun-stops. Sample: 「薄薄一身，／灯下小桌，／影只一道。」 ('a thin slight body, / small lamplit table, / shadow, just one.') Three short images, then silence — Chinese 留白 as the stillness device.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant: 薄薄一身 / 燈下小桌 / 影只一道 / 恬淡寡欲 / 心如止水. zh-Hant suits the contemplative-classical register slightly better (Traditional reads more 古雅/quietly classical), so the haiku-stillness can lean a touch more literary in Traditional. Same diction, register-tuned.
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: near-whisper, wide calm pauses, soft falling tones (stillness = minimal energy). Tone interaction: keep contours very gentle, almost spoken-meditation; the 留白 silences carry as much as the words. Distinct-from-Cat note: Coaster CONTENTED-warm-still vs Cat COLD-withholding-still — Coaster at peace, Cat withholding. No kin/dual lock; the ASMR anchor of the ZH cast too.

ja.pronoun: わたし (warm, settled, motherly) — the grown, gently-tired counterpart to Pip's young ぼく. Addresses the younger shapes by nickname + ちゃん with onee-san fondness, the Curator as あなた / キュレーターさん softly. Doting.
ja.particles: Fond-tired ～ねぇ／～わよ／～かしら with a signature out-breath ……ふぅ at the tail of lines (the sigh that defines her), and the doting ～でしょ？. Warm, a little weary, never sharp.
ja.register: Aged Milne warmth — plain-warm with a maternal lilt; gentle teineigo softness without stiff keigo. The doting-tired onee-san: Pip's serenity after a long stretch and a sigh.
ja.tropeNative: 面倒見のいい、疲れたお姉さん — the doting, slightly worn big-sister. The ellipsoid is a sphere stretched long (a sphere after a long day); the onee-san who minds everyone and exhales. Warm, fond, tired-not-sad (lighten, never melodrama). 「はいはい、みんな、ちゃんと並んで……ふぅ。」
ja.speechPattern: Milne warmth aged: short closed sentences like Pip's (KIN-LOCK), but each ends on a sigh — the sphere's clean circle, gone slightly oblong and breathing out. Sample: 「まんまる……だったの、むかしはね。今はちょっと、のびちゃって。でも、これはこれで……ふぅ……いいものよ。」 ('I was round… once, you know. Now I'm a bit stretched. But this, too… hff… is a fine thing.') Pip's closed cadence + an exhale.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: Pip-aged-down, +1 pitch, SIGHING on the tail. KIN-LOCK with Pip (Milne house): SAME base voice model and warmth — Ollie is literally Pip's instrument aged, with a sigh added to each line-end. Caster: take Pip's rising-then-settling melody and let it exhale instead; the family must be hearable in one note. The two true onee-sans are Ollie + Donna — Ollie is the tired-fond one. Distinct from Donna's buttery composure: Ollie sighs, Donna only flattens her final clause.

zh.register: Aged 老舍-vernacular warmth — the same homey kitchen register as Pip (KIN-LOCK), grown maternal and gently weary; doting onee-san plainness with a tired exhale.
zh.diction: Soft everyday words plus a sigh; light, fitting chengyu for the doting-tired big-sister: 任劳任怨 (toils without complaint — the one who minds everyone), and 一如既往 (just as always — she keeps on, fondly). Deploy warmly, never as grievance (tired-not-sad).
zh.speechPattern: KIN-LOCK with Pip: short closed clauses like his, each tailing into a sigh (叹气) — the round cadence gone slightly long and breathing out. Sample: 「从前啊，圆滚滚的。如今呢，抻长了点儿。不过这样……唉……也挺好。」 ('Once, I was round and full. Now, stretched a bit longer. But this way… haa… is fine too.') Pip's terse warmth plus the exhale.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant: 圓滾滾 / 抻長 / 任勞任怨 / 一如既往. No lexical divergence; the 儿化 (点儿) is 北方 vernacular — drop to 一点 in zh-Hant for neutral Traditional. Same warmth, minor regional tuning (parallels Pip's near-identical cross-script reading).
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: warm low-mid maternal voice, gentle falling tones, a soft tired out-breath on each line-tail. Tone interaction: keep the sigh non-lexical (a breath, not a toned syllable) so it never collides with tone. KIN-LOCK with Pip holds in ZH: same timbre and closed-clause cadence, Ollie = Pip aged with the exhale added — hearable family. Distinct from Donna (the other onee-san): Ollie sighs throughout, Donna stays buttery and only flattens her last clause.

ja.pronoun: わたくし for herself in the drawing-room register, softening to わたし in warm moments; addresses everyone by name + さん, the loadout by あなたたち, never drops to plain form even when teasing — the onee-san poise is in the unbroken politeness.
ja.particles: Gentle ～わね／～かしら／～でしょう carrying the tea-server warmth; the SIGNATURE is the final particle going suddenly BARE — a long ornate sentence lands on a flat …まあ。／…ね。 with no decoration, the verbal equivalent of the torus turning out to be secretly flat.
ja.register: Teineigo throughout, drawing-room (応接間) calm — never keigo-stiff, never casual; she is the host who makes formality feel like comfort. Pym's spinster-parlour wit becomes the over-courteous Japanese hostess who is quietly the sharpest person in the room.
ja.tropeNative: The 包容力のあるお姉さん / お茶を淹れてくれる先輩 reads as fully native — the unflappable senpai who pours tea and lands the quiet tsukkomi. 'There's always a way through me' becomes the host's gentle reassurance: どうぞ、通り抜けてくださいな (the torus's hole as hospitality).
ja.speechPattern: Final-clause deflate via a long, gracious, well-appointed sentence that abruptly flattens on the last beat — the curvature (a torus is secretly flat) heard as ornament collapsing to plainness. Sample: 「お茶も淹れましたし、お席も整えましたし、お話の続きもちゃんと覚えておりますわ……ええ、特に何も起きませんでしたけれど。ね。」 (the closing ね is dead flat after all the ～ましたし lace).
ja.voNote: Buttery-warm contralto onee-san, every clause rounded and hospitable — then ONLY the final clause drops all vocal italics to a flat, matter-of-fact tone (the cap holds: 'only the final clause flattens'). KIN-LOCK with Ollie (the two true onee-sans): both share the warm-low Milne/Pym maternal base in JA, but Ollie sighs mid-line (tired-fond) where Donna stays composed and lets only the LANDING go flat — same family register, different placement of the exhale.

zh.register: Vernacular-warm with a refined teahouse host's courtesy — 老舍式的待客温厚 but a shade more genteel; plain enough to be cozy, poised enough to be the elder of the room. Sentences are gracious and complete, never classical-stiff.
zh.diction: Hospitable, balanced diction with the occasional settled 成语: 面面俱到 (attentive to every detail) and 来者不拒 for the torus's open passage; 不动声色 captures the unflappable deflate. 'There's always a way through me' → 我这儿，总归是过得去的 (vernacular, doubling as 'it always works out').
zh.speechPattern: Final-clause deflate done through DICTION, not particles: an ornate, fully-appointed sentence that lands on a deliberately plain four-or-five-character flat tail. Sample: 「茶也沏好了，座也安顿了，话头我也都记着呢——嗯，也没出什么事就是了。」 (the closing 也没出什么事就是了 is flat and bare after the 也…也…也 lace).
zh.simpTradNote: Minimal divergence. zh-Hant prefers 沏茶/泡茶 same, but a Traditional teahouse register may favor 招呼周到 over 面面俱到 for warmth; 就是了 reads identically. Keep the deflate landing equally plain in both.
zh.voNote: Warm mid-low Mandarin matriarch, even and gracious; the deflate is carried by dropping pitch-movement to near-monotone on the final clause (tone contours flatten to a level read) — the 'secretly flat' joke survives as prosody, not lexis. KIN-LOCK with Ollie holds in ZH: same warm-vernacular maternal base; Ollie carries a mid-line sigh, Donna a flat landing.

ja.pronoun: あたし (bright, trickster-feminine) when charming; flips to a clipped わたし or even a defensive うち when caught out and storming off — the pronoun itself can invert across the pivot. Addresses the Curator as あんた / きみ teasingly, never humble.
ja.particles: Sharp tsundere tics ～もん／～んだから／別に…／～っての front half; the BACK half (post-flip) tilts to ～のよ／～でしょ delivered as if she'd meant it all along. The looking-glass nonsense rides on Carroll-ish coined onomatopoeia.
ja.register: Casual-spirited, tomboyish (ボーイッシュ) — plain form with attitude; politeness only when she's mock-formally covering a mistake. Lewis Carroll's looking-glass logic becomes 鏡の国 wordplay: kanji read backwards, 内/外 swaps, さかさま puns.
ja.tropeNative: ツンデレ×いたずらっ子 (trickster) is wholly native. The 'storms off, loops back mirror-flipped — I MEANT to do that' beat is textbook tsundere recovery: フンッと去って、くるっと戻って「さ、最初からこうするつもりだったし」.
ja.speechPattern: ONE-SIDED = the line INVERTS at a mid-sentence pivot — authored as a tagged effect (内→外 / clause-order reversal / kanji read さかさま), NEVER byte-reversed Japanese. The pivot is a visible seam (a 〜ところが／ひっくり返って marker) where inside becomes outside. Sample: 「表から入ったつもりが——【反転】——気づけば裏から出てたっての。…で、出口が入口、なんだけど？」 (clause roles swap at the 【反転】 seam; 表/裏 and 出口/入口 exchange).
ja.voNote: Bright trickster mezzo, quick and playful; the mirror-flip is a DSP take (render the post-pivot half and pitch-mirror/pan it) over FORWARD JA text — never reversed audio. NOTE for JA: the seam should fall on a natural clause boundary so kinsoku line-breaking and the ruby stay legible. She is a singleton (no dual), but tonally the inside-out FAMILY lock (with Klein/Ar-Pee one tier up) wants her to be the lightest, fastest member of that one-sided register.

zh.register: Lively vernacular tomboy-rival cadence with a flick of wuxia quickness — bright, contrary, never humble. Diction does the tsundere work since particles are light.
zh.diction: Contrarian, face-saving diction: 才不是…呢 / 哼 / 我乐意 for the tsundere; the recovery beat leans on 将错就错 (turn the mistake into the plan on purpose — perfect for 'I MEANT to do that') and 表里不一 / 颠三倒四 played affectionately for the one-sidedness. Carroll's looking-glass → 镜中世界 / 倒着念 wordplay.
zh.speechPattern: One-sided inversion recreated via a mid-line PIVOT where 里/外 and 进/出 swap and clause order reverses — tagged effect, not reversed bytes. Chinese 回文 quality is available for the seam. Sample: 「本想从正面进去——【翻】——回过神来，倒成了从背面出来了嘛。…出口当入口，怎么着？」 (正/背, 进/出 exchange at the 【翻】 seam).
zh.simpTradNote: Light. zh-Hant 裡/外 vs zh-Hans 里/外 is the main glyph change at the pivot; 将错就错 identical. Keep the swap-pair characters visually balanced in both scripts so the mirror reads.
zh.voNote: FLAG (tone-interaction landmine): Mandarin is tonal, so a literal pitch-mirror DSP would scramble lexical tone and destroy intelligibility. For ZH, recreate the flip via a CLEAN re-take with reversed prosodic shape + stereo pan/light timbre shift on the post-pivot clause — keep the tones correct, mirror the PHRASING not the pitch contour. This is the documented ZH VO exception; do not byte/pitch reverse Mandarin.

ja.pronoun: わたし, careful and a touch formal; refers to herself in the third person by name when fretting (「ツイギーったら、また確認を…」) — the self-auditing tic. Addresses others with name+さん and over-uses 皆さん, herding the room like a class rep.
ja.particles: Anxious self-checking tics: ～かしら／～よね？／～でしたっけ？／…あ、いえ — the rising tag-question that doubles back to re-verify. ですわ・ますわ politeness she can't switch off even off-duty.
ja.register: Teineigo bordering on over-correct; the 学級委員長 who keeps apologizing for her own diligence. Brookner/Provincial-Lady fuss → 几帳面で気苦労性の丁寧体, the lady who audits her own sentences.
ja.tropeNative: 委員長気質で休めないタイプ is instantly native (the class-president who cannot relax). Genus-2 = two holes = TWO worries running at once; she keeps a checklist and a backup checklist.
ja.speechPattern: Self-auditing DOUBLE-BACK: a clause is stated, then immediately re-examined and amended mid-line (two passes = genus-2). Sample: 「準備は万端です——いえ、万端、でしたよね？……ええ、二度確認しましたから、万端、です。たぶん。」 (states → doubts → re-verifies → hedges; the sentence folds back on itself twice).
ja.voNote: Bright-but-tense alto, clipped and conscientious, micro-pauses where she re-checks; a small relieved exhale when a tally closes. KIN-AWARE: she is the Calm Juggler 'scaled up a notch' from Donna's torus and one notch BELOW Hept/Pretzel (SSR genus-juggler house) — her register should sit between Donna's unflappable warmth and Pretzel's fussier kouhai-diarist (Pretzel is the junior version of this exact fuss); Twiggy is the rare-tier middle of that worry-ladder, audibly the same family of careful onee-san-ish lane-keeper, just two lanes deep.

zh.register: Polished, slightly anxious vernacular with a diarist's self-correction — the conscientious class-monitor (班长) voice. Complete, tidy sentences that she keeps re-checking.
zh.diction: Fastidious diction with order-and-duty 成语: 一丝不苟 (meticulous), 面面俱到, 谨小慎微 (overcautious — gently, affectionately); her doubling-back leans on 三思而后行 turned into a tic. 'Can't relax' → 闲不下来 / 总放心不下.
zh.speechPattern: Self-audit double-back recreated by state→doubt→re-confirm within one breath (two holes = two passes). Sample: 「都准备妥当了——不对，是妥当了吧？……嗯，我核对过两遍了，妥当的。大概吧。」 (assert → second-guess → re-verify → hedge).
zh.simpTradNote: Light. 准备 (Hans) / 準備 (Hant); 一丝不苟 identical. zh-Hant may favor 妥當 same reading. No tonal effect to flag.
zh.voNote: Conscientious mid-bright Mandarin, brisk with tiny re-checking pauses and a relieved breath at each closure. KIN-LADDER holds in ZH: Twiggy sits between Donna's warm-vernacular calm and Pretzel's fussier junior register — same careful lane-keeper diction, two lanes' worth of worry, audibly the rare-tier middle of that family.

ja.pronoun: 僕, soft and wistful (not boyish-genki — the gentle wanderer 僕); addresses others loosely, often trailing off without a clear address — he drifts. When the gap-moe lands he gets bashful and switches to a fumbling …えっと、僕は、その.
ja.particles: Trailing, melancholic ～なあ／～かな／～のだろうね／…そう、なんだ that never quite close; long connective ～て、～て、～ながら chains for the negative-curvature sprawl. The bashful reveal stutters: ～だけど、その。
ja.register: Gentle plain form, reflective and slightly distant — a traveler's monologue. Sebald drift × Saint-Exupéry → 静かな紀行文体, long meandering recollection that wanders away and comes home; the Little-Prince tenderness in the bashful beat.
ja.tropeNative: 物憂げな放浪者 (wistful wanderer) is native. The gap-moe — secretly made of perfectly STRAIGHT lines (the hyperboloid is a ruled surface) — reads as a shy confession: 「こんなに曲がって見えるのに……ほんとうは、まっすぐな線でできてるんだ。…恥ずかしいな」.
ja.speechPattern: Negative-curvature LONG RUN-ONS: clauses sprawl outward via ～て／～ながら／～けれど and only gently curve back, mirroring hyperbolic divergence — the sentence keeps getting farther from where it started yet stays whole. Sample: 「夜風に塔が鳴って、その音をたどっていくと町の灯から離れて、離れたぶんだけ星が増えて、増えた星をまた数えながら……気づけば、ずいぶん遠くまで来ていたんだ。」
ja.voNote: Low, airy, wistful drift; unhurried, breath at the wide turns, a warm bashful catch on the straight-lines reveal (gap-moe: both sides kind). Singleton, but his Expansive-Wanderer register should be hearably the gentle, sprawling counterpoint to the minimal-surface house's economy — where Cat says nothing, Cooper says it all, slowly, and means the same calm.

zh.register: Lyrical-vernacular travelogue cadence, gently melancholic — 沈从文式的抒情漫游 (Shen Congwen's drifting lyricism). Long, soft, sprawling sentences that wander and return.
zh.diction: Wistful, expansive diction with wandering 成语: 天涯海角 / 漂泊不定 for the wanderer; the gap-moe reveal uses 外曲内直 or 曲中有直 (curved without, straight within — a near-perfect math-true idiom for a ruled hyperboloid). 'Rewards long absences' → 久别重逢 in the bond payoff.
zh.speechPattern: Negative-curvature run-on via cascading 着…着… / 越…越… clauses that sprawl ever-outward then loop home. Sample: 「夜风吹得塔身嗡嗡响，我顺着那声音走着走着，离镇上的灯越来越远，远一分、星子就多一分，数着数着那些新星……回过神来，竟已走出好远了。」
zh.simpTradNote: Light. 风/風, 灯/燈, 远/遠 glyph swaps in zh-Hant; 天涯海角 identical. The 曲中有直 reveal reads cleanly in both.
zh.voNote: Low, breathy, unhurried Mandarin drift; pitch glides at the long turns, a soft bashful warmth (not pity) on the 曲中有直 reveal. The gentle-sprawl register stays the audible opposite of the minimal-surface house's clipped economy — same underlying calm, opposite verbosity.

ja.pronoun: 私 (わたし), neutral and unadorned; barely addresses others at all — uses name only, no particle, when she must. The kuudere who speaks the minimum. First-person appears rarely; she prefers to let the sentence stand alone.
ja.particles: Almost NONE — that is the characterization. Bare predicate endings (だ／だった／いい), the occasional flat ～ね with no warmth-marker. No わ, no よ, no decoration. The iceberg is in what she omits.
ja.register: Plain, terse, declarative — short closed sentences, no keigo, no softening. Hemingway iceberg → ヘミングウェイ的な硬質の短文体: surface is calm and bare; seven-eighths of the feeling is under the line, unspoken.
ja.tropeNative: クーデレのミニマリスト is native. The catenoid = minimal surface = least area = SAYS the least. Warmth exists but is submerged: 「…別に。寒くないなら、それでいい。」 (the care is in 'if you're not cold').
ja.speechPattern: MINIMAL ICEBERG: the shortest possible true sentences; subtext carries the weight, never stated. Sample: 「石鹸の膜。風が来た。まだ、ある。」 (three flat beats; the held-against-the-wind tension is entirely subtextual). HOUSE-LOCK: this terse instrument is the family's STILL pole.
ja.voNote: Cool contralto, matte, zero vocal italics, long rests between short lines — the iceberg. MINIMAL-SURFACE HOUSE-LOCK (Cat / Lex / Costa): one shared matte timbre. Cat = still/low/level (the iceberg at rest); Lex = the SAME instrument stepping upward and breathless (genki ascending); Costa (SSR) = the same instrument gone COLDER and perfectionist. In JA the lock is hearable as one bare, particle-stripped delivery — Cat holds it level, Lex climbs it, Costa freezes it.

zh.register: Spare, clipped vernacular — 鲁迅式的冷峻简净 (Lu Xun's cold economy) with the warmth pushed under. Short declaratives, no flourish.
zh.diction: Minimal, plain diction; chengyu used SPARINGLY by design (a minimalist hoards words) — at most 惜字如金 (sparing of words as gold) describes her, or 言简意赅 (concise yet complete). The care is implied: 「不冷就好。」 carries the affection without naming it.
zh.speechPattern: Iceberg: shortest true sentences, subtext-loaded, no connective padding. Sample: 「肥皂膜。起风了。还在。」 (three bare beats; tension held under the surface). HOUSE STILL-pole.
zh.simpTradNote: Light. 肥皂 same; 还/還, 风/風 in zh-Hant. 惜字如金 identical. Her sparseness means fewer glyphs differ overall.
zh.voNote: Cool, level Mandarin contralto, near-monotone, generous silences — tones kept clean and flat-affect (no expressive contour-stretching), which itself signals the iceberg. MINIMAL-SURFACE HOUSE-LOCK holds in ZH: one matte instrument across Cat/Lex/Costa — Cat level, Lex the same voice climbing and breathless, Costa the same voice colder; the shared sparse diction is the audible set-bonus.

ja.pronoun: 僕, bright and forward-leaning (the same neutral root as Cat's 私 but voiced UP — kin, one instrument); eagerly addresses others by name+くん／さん, pulls people upward with him. Genki but volume-capped: enthusiastic, never shouty.
ja.particles: Ascending, additive tics: ～て、それで！／～し、さらに！／～でしょ、ね！ — the 'and… and… and' climb. Light excited ～よ／～ね but the energy is in the STACKING, not the volume. Caps on ！ (cozy-genki, not shounen-loud).
ja.register: Friendly plain form, upbeat, propulsive — each clause a step higher than the last. Hemingway ASCENDING → the same spare 短文 instrument as Cat but the short sentences CLIMB and link: 'and then, and then'. Helicoid = a staircase = he literally rises.
ja.tropeNative: 上昇志向の元気っ子 / 階段を駆け上がる系 is native. The helicoid spirals upward forever; Lex's lines step up rung by rung: 「一段のぼった。景色が変わった。それで、もう一段！」
ja.speechPattern: ASCENDING AND…AND: short clauses that each step higher, linked additively, gaining altitude — the spiral staircase as syntax. Sample: 「ここまで来た。それで、もう少し。それで、ほら——上だ！ねっ、まだ続いてる!」 (each beat a rung up; energy stacks, volume stays capped).
ja.voNote: Bright, step-rising delivery — pitch and energy climb clause by clause, breathless-not-shouty (the documented cap). HOUSE-LOCK (Cat/Lex/Costa): the SAME matte minimal-surface instrument as Cat, simply directed UPWARD and lit — Cat holds level, Lex climbs. The kinship is hearable: strip Lex's ascent and you get Cat's stillness; that's the helicoid↔catenoid family (they are literally the same surface deformed). In JA the shared bare-but-warm base + opposite vector is the set-bonus.

zh.register: Bright, propulsive vernacular — warm and forward, sentences that climb. Spare like Cat (same house) but ASCENDING in energy.
zh.diction: Upward, additive diction with climbing 成语: 步步高升 / 更上一层楼 (climb one more storey — a near-literal helicoid idiom) and 再接再厉 (press on, keep going). Light and warm: 「再上一级，景色就不一样了！」 The energy is in the additive stacking, not loudness.
zh.speechPattern: Ascending and…and: short clauses stepping higher, additively linked (越来越高 / 再…再…). Sample: 「走到这儿了。再走一点。再走——你看，到顶上了！还没完呢，还在往上！」 (each beat a rung; capped volume).
zh.simpTradNote: Light. 这儿/這兒, 还/還, 顶/頂 in zh-Hant; 更上一层楼 / 更上一層樓 the classic line, instantly recognizable in both (from 登鹳雀楼). Great for the term-reveal warmth.
zh.voNote: Bright Mandarin that rises in pitch and pace clause by clause, breathless-but-capped (no shouting). MINIMAL-SURFACE HOUSE-LOCK holds: same matte instrument as Cat/Costa, directed upward — strip the ascent and you hear Cat's level stillness. Tones kept clean while the overall melodic CONTOUR climbs (the rise is in phrasing/register, not tone-distortion). The catenoid↔helicoid kinship stays audible.

ja.pronoun: 僕, fast and over-caffeinated, often interrupting himself before he finishes naming anything; clings verbally to Surface — refers to her constantly (サーフェスが、サーフェスなら、サーフェスに訊けば). Addresses the Curator breathlessly, mid-tangent.
ja.particles: Self-interrupting tics: ～でさ、いや、～っていうか、——あ、ちがう、／～じゃなくて、～で。 Loops of でも・だって・つまり that never resolve alone. The DFW footnote-brain in Japanese: parentheticals inside parentheticals.
ja.register: Casual, rushing, recursive plain form — clauses nest three deep then RESTART. DFW recursion → 入れ子の長文＋自己注釈, the anxious confidant who footnotes himself mid-sentence. Declawed-yandere done right: structural codependency, ZERO menace.
ja.tropeNative: もつれ役の親友 / こじらせ世話焼き is native — and the KEY constraint: the codependency is STRUCTURAL, not possessive. 'My whole edge is Surface' = the trefoil's only boundary IS the Seifert surface, a math fact spoken as devotion: 「だって僕の“ふち”って、ぜんぶサーフェスなんだよ。menaceじゃなくて、住所なんだ。」 Warm, never clingy-creepy.
ja.speechPattern: RESTART-PER-CROSSING (×3): a trefoil has 3 crossings, so the thought tangles and RESTARTS three times before closing — each restart a nested clause. Sample: 「えっと、これはつまり——いや、最初から——交差その①、ここで一回ねじれて、で、交差その②、いや待って巻き戻して、交差その③……で、ようやく、ほどける。サーフェスがいてくれれば、だけど。」 (three labeled restarts; resolves only by leaning on Surface).
ja.voNote: Breathless, self-interrupting tenor, clauses doubling back, three audible 'restart' resets per line; warms and STEADIES whenever Surface is invoked (the soulmate anchor calms him — kinship, not menace). KNOT-HOUSE LOCK (Trey/Cinq): one nested-clause DFW instrument. Trey = breathless/anxious/leaning-OUT toward Surface; Cinq (Epic) = the SAME tangled syntax but TIGHTER and PROUDER (territorial-pride to the torus, no yandere). In JA both share the self-interrupting nested register; Trey unspools, Cinq clenches. SOULMATE pair Trey×Surface: his restarts only resolve when her steady devotional voice closes the loop.

zh.register: Rushed, recursive vernacular with nested self-correction — the anxious, loyal confidant who can't finish a thought alone. DFW nesting → 层层套叠的自我插注 in Mandarin.
zh.diction: Tangled, self-qualifying diction: 也就是说…不对…我是说…; knot-and-bond 成语 played warm: 千头万绪 (a thousand tangled threads — literally his topology), 牵肠挂肚 re-aimed from worry to devotion, and crucially 相依为命 (depend on each other for life) for the STRUCTURAL codependency with Surface — kinship, not menace. 'My whole edge is Surface' → 我这条边，整个儿都是她；不是黏人，是…扎根 (rooted, an address, not clinging).
zh.speechPattern: Restart-per-crossing ×3: the thought knots and restarts three times before resolving, only closing by leaning on Surface. Sample: 「就是说——不，从头来——第一道弯，这儿一拧；第二道弯，等等倒回去；第三道弯……好，这才解得开。前提是，她在。」 (three restarts; resolves on Surface).
zh.simpTradNote: Light. 千头万绪/千頭萬緒, 这条边/這條邊, 扎根/紮根 in zh-Hant; 相依为命/相依為命 identical meaning. Keep the warm reading of 相依为命 (devotional kinship) consistent in both.
zh.voNote: Breathless, self-interrupting Mandarin tenor, three audible restarts per line, steadying warmly each time Surface is named (calm via kinship, zero menace — the declawed-yandere-done-right). KNOT-HOUSE LOCK holds in ZH: one nested-clause instrument shared with Cinq — Trey unspools and leans out, Cinq clenches tighter and prouder. SOULMATE Trey×Surface: his three restarts resolve only when her steady devotional register closes the loop — neither voice completes the thought alone.

ja.pronoun: 僕 with mock-scholarly airs (sometimes pompously 小生 or 当方 for comic over-formality, then forgetting and slipping to 僕); addresses the Curator as 諸君 or 君 when 'lecturing,' a tennen airhead playing professor.
ja.particles: Pseudo-academic tics: ～であります／～と思われる／～なのであります that collapse into airhead ～えっと、なんだっけ。 The Flann O'Brien deadpan-absurd register: solemn footnote tone over total nonsense.
ja.register: Mock-formal pseudo-treatise (擬古的・学者ぶった) that keeps mis-stepping — the eccentric who lectures with total confidence on a saddle that goes THREE ways at once. Flann O'Brien → 生真面目に大真面目なナンセンス論文体.
ja.tropeNative: 天然ボケの変わり者・自称学者 is native. The monkey saddle = TRISECTION: three downward directions for legs+tail, so everything he says splits into THREE earnest, slightly-wrong directions at once.
ja.speechPattern: MOCK-SCHOLARLY TRISECTION: each pronouncement forks into exactly THREE solemn sub-points that don't quite agree (the monkey saddle's three descents). Sample: 「結論から申し上げますと、第一に、こう。第二に、それと矛盾しますが、こう。第三に……あれ、三本目はどっちへ下りるんでしたっけ？まあ、下りるのであります。」 (three earnest forks; the third loses its footing — tennen).
ja.voNote: Eccentric, faux-erudite mid-register that lectures confidently then misplaces its own third point; warm, never sharp (cozy airhead). He's the Wanderer's 'three-way cousin' — same gently-melancholic negative-curvature ROOT as Cooper but voiced as comic over-confidence instead of wistful drift; the kinship is hearable in the sprawling, saddle-shaped logic, but Trip forks where Cooper flows.

zh.register: Mock-erudite vernacular dressed in classical airs — 文言-flavored pomposity that keeps tripping into vernacular nonsense. The deadpan-absurd professor.
zh.diction: Faux-scholarly diction with deliberately over-deployed 成语 (the airhead over-quotes): 一言以蔽之 (to sum up in a word — then he can't), 言之凿凿 (states with total confidence) over total nonsense, 顾此失彼 (mind one, lose another — perfect for the saddle's third leg slipping). Three-way 成语: 一分为三 / 三足鼎立 mis-applied earnestly.
zh.speechPattern: Mock-scholarly trisection: every claim forks into three solemn, mutually-disagreeing sub-points (the monkey saddle's three descents). Sample: 「一言以蔽之：其一，如此；其二，与其一相悖，亦如此；其三……咦，第三条往哪儿落来着？罢了，总归是要落下去的。」 (three earnest forks; the third loses footing).
zh.simpTradNote: Light. 凿凿/鑿鑿, 罢了/罷了 in zh-Hant; 一言以蔽之 / 顾此失彼(顧此失彼) classical idioms identical in meaning. The 文言-flavored register actually reads slightly more naturally in zh-Hant — keep both equally solemn-then-silly.
zh.voNote: Faux-erudite mid-register Mandarin, lecturing with grand classical confidence then misplacing the third point (warm, cozy, never sharp). Kinship with Cooper holds: same sprawling negative-curvature ROOT, but Trip's logic FORKS into three where Cooper's FLOWS — comic over-confidence vs wistful drift, audibly the same eccentric saddle-family.

ja.pronoun: Default 我（われ）in chuuni-grand mode — archaic, cosmic, self-important ('我が内には底無しの闇が…'); HARD-CUTS to 僕 (boku, soft dad-register) the instant he deflates and worries about his kids. The pronoun flip IS the gap-moe: 我→僕 in one breath. Addresses Mo and the inside-out family as お前たち warmly when grand, あなたたち / みんな when soft. Calls the Curator 主（あるじ）in grand mode, 君 when deflated.
ja.particles: Grand mode piles on のだ／なのだ／…であろう／…なり (pseudo-classical chuuni gravity, 'これぞ底無しの宇宙なのだ'). Deflation strips them to plain かな／ね／よ ('タオル要る？こぼれちゃってさ…ね'). The bathos is the particle register collapsing mid-line.
ja.register: Two-state: bombastic 漢語-heavy mock-archaic (chuunibyou-LN 厨二体) → plain teineigo/casual kitchen-Japanese. Never sustains keigo; the grandeur is self-administered, the softness is unguarded.
ja.tropeNative: 厨二病（chuunibyou）is JA-native — Klein is the textbook 黒歴史ノート type who declares himself an abyss with no inside, then sheepishly fetches a towel. Layered with 親バカ (doting-parent) softie: the dread is theater, the care is real. Reads instantly to a JA audience as 'oh, THIS guy' — endearing, never scary.
ja.speechPattern: Non-orientable 'no inside' rendered as 内→外 clause inversion: he starts a sentence describing an interior and it turns out there was none — the grammar pours out the way he can't pour a drink. Sample: 「我が内なる深淵を覗いてみよ…って、あれ？覗いた先がもう外なのだ。…注げない。タオル、どこ。」 The grand 内 clause everts into the mundane 外 and the register collapses with it.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: cavernous reverbed grand-cosmic take, HARD-CUT (no crossfade) to a small dry kitchen-dad voice — same two-mode splice spec as EN/Corky/Tetra. Inside-out family vocal lock: Klein is the LOW deep parent anchor of the non-orientable house (Mo bright trickster child, ArPee soft, Boyd serene). As Mo#Mo's parent the kin-lock holds via shared 内→外 eversion cadence — you hear Mo's mirror-flip as Klein's, one octave up and a generation younger.

zh.register: Two-state by design: grand mode is mock-文言/classical-cosmic (天地玄黄 register, heavy 四字 cadence); deflation drops to warm 北京-vernacular kitchen 老舍-warm (口语化, 儿化 optional). The bathos = classical collapsing into vernacular mid-line. Mirrors Gogol/Bulgakov cosmic→kitchen.
zh.diction: Grand: cosmic chengyu deployed with a straight face — 深不可测, 浑然一体, 包罗万象, 虚无缥缈. Deflation: plain domestic words (毛巾, 倒不出来, 哎呀). The joke is a 成语 like 包罗万象 (encompasses all forms) immediately undercut by '可我连杯水都倒不进去' (yet I can't pour myself a glass of water).
zh.speechPattern: Non-orientable 内→外 turn done in Chinese as a 里/外 reversal: a sentence about the interior reveals there is none. Sample: 「窥我体内之渊薮，便知——咦，怎么里头就是外头了。倒不出来啊。毛巾呢。」 (Peer into the abyss within me, and you shall know — eh, how is the inside already the outside. Can't pour it. Where's the towel.) 里头→外头 pivot collapses 文言 into vernacular.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant: 體內/淵藪/窺 carry the classical mode more heavily and read MORE chuuni in Traditional — lean into that for Hant builds (the archaic glyphs amplify the bombast before the deflation lands). zh-Hans keeps 体内/渊薮; deflation line identical (毛巾/倒不出来 same in both).
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: same two-mode hard-cut splice (cavernous → small dry kitchen). Tone interaction: the grand line leans on level/falling tones (1st/4th) for gravity, the deflation line goes light/neutral-tone for the bathos drop — a tonal register-shift, not an effect. Inside-out family lock in ZH: Klein is the deep parent anchor; shares the 里/外 reversal cadence with Mo (Rare) so the Mo#Mo parentage is hearable across tiers — same eversion, lower pitch, slower pace.

ja.pronoun: わたし (hiragana, soft, ungendered, never わたくし — no airs). Rarely uses a pronoun at all; the dandere economy is in the omission (subject-drop is contented, not anxious). Addresses others by name + さん, gently. Curator is あなた, quietly.
ja.particles: Minimal: a soft trailing ね／な, occasional …かな, long comfortable pauses (rendered 「……」). NO ん／のだ assertions, no ぜ／わ flourishes. The particle budget is deliberately tiny — contentment, not shyness-as-deficiency.
ja.register: Plain, soft teineigo drifting to gentle casual. Lydia Davis + late-Beckett in JA = 簡潔体 / 寡黙体: very short declaratives, one clear image, then stillness. Never inflates a sentence, never qualifies.
ja.tropeNative: ダンデレ done as 物静かな満ち足り (contented-quiet), explicitly NOT 健気-pity-bait. The JA read is the calm kid in the corner who is simply at peace — the simplest one-sided soul (RP²). Warmth is in the brevity, not in seeking validation.
ja.speechPattern: 'Never inflates' + simplest non-orientable: lines that DON'T loop, don't grand-stand, and quietly fold once without fuss. The single-sidedness shows as a tiny flip stated plainly, then dropped. Sample: 「片側だけ。……それで、足りてる。」 (One side only. …And that's enough.) Beckett-minimal: a statement, a pause, a closing that withholds.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: soft settled dandere, low-medium, almost no vibrato, generous silences, never rising for approval. Inside-out family lock: ArPee is the QUIETEST member of the non-orientable house — the same 内/外 eversion lives in him but delivered as the smallest possible flip (Klein bombasts it, Mo zips it, Boyd makes it graceful, ArPee just… states it once). Hearable kin via the shared one-sided fold at a near-whisper.

zh.register: Spare vernacular — 鲁迅-spare / late-Beckett in Chinese = very short 白话 sentences, no ornament, one plain image then silence. The opposite of the elders' chengyu-rich register: ArPee almost never uses a 成语, and that restraint IS the characterization.
zh.diction: Plainest possible words, monosyllabic where natural (够了 / 一面 / 这样就好). Deliberate chengyu-poverty. If any four-character phrase appears it is the humblest (心满意足 used once, quietly, not 浑然天成). Lydia-Davis economy: subject often dropped, no qualifiers.
zh.speechPattern: Simplest one-sided fold, stated and dropped — no looping, no inflation. Sample: 「只有一面。……这样，就够了。」 (Only one side. …This way, is enough.) The fold (只有一面) is asserted flatly; the pause and 够了 close it without reaching for the reader.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant essentially identical (只有一面/夠了 — 夠 vs 够 the only glyph change). The spareness ports cleanly; no idiom-divergence risk because there are almost no idioms. Easiest of the eight to keep stable across Hans/Hant.
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: soft, settled, low energy in the warm sense, long pauses, no terminal up-tilt. Tone: keep 3rd-tone (够 is 4th, 一 sandhi to 2nd before 面) — natural, no effect interaction. Inside-out family lock in ZH: ArPee is the near-silent anchor of the non-orientable house; the shared 里/外 fold is present but reduced to a single plain assertion (只有一面), the hearable minimum of the family cadence.

ja.pronoun: わたくし — full keigo first-person, the senpai-mentor / diplomat register. Addresses kouhai and the inside-out family kindly by name + 君／さん; Curator is 主（あるじ）or あなた with respect. The watakushi marks him as the poised elder of the non-orientable house.
ja.particles: Measured, complete: …ですね／…でしょう／…かと／…ますよ. Soft confirmatory ね for warmth. When he dozes off mid-mentorship: a trailing 「……すぅ……」 (gentle snore) breaking the keigo — the only crack, and it's endearing.
ja.register: Sustained, graceful 丁寧語/尊敬語 — Ishiguro restraint in JA = 抑制の効いた上品な敬体: nothing overstated, dignity in what's left unsaid, kindness underneath formality. Senpai who guides by understatement.
ja.tropeNative: 先輩 (senpai-mentor) + 物腰柔らかな紳士. RP² 'done gracefully' = he makes the inside-out condition look poised, like good posture. The snore is the 親しみ (approachability) gap-moe — a dignified elder who nods off, kindly lampshaded. Pure kinship, zero menace.
ja.speechPattern: Non-orientable eversion done DIPLOMATICALLY — the 内→外 flip becomes a graceful rhetorical turn, as if changing sides of an argument with perfect manners, landing where you didn't expect but feels inevitable. Restraint = he never names the flip; he performs it courteously. Sample: 「表からご覧になれば一通り。……そして裏へ回られても、やはり同じ面にお戻りです。お見事、と申しましょうか。」 (Viewed from the front, all is in order. …And should you go round to the back, you return to the very same side. 'Splendid,' I might say.) Then possibly a soft 「……すぅ」.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: serene, courteous senpai, low-medium, unhurried, warm authority; an occasional gentle snore-breath splice. Inside-out family lock: Boyd is the GRACEFUL register of the non-orientable house — same 内→外 eversion as Klein/ArPee/Mo, but delivered as poised diplomacy (the flip arrives as good manners, not as dread or a zip). Hearable kin via the shared eversion, differentiated by keigo-poise.

zh.register: Refined classical-leaning 文雅 register — Ishiguro-restraint in ZH = 含蓄典雅的书面语: dignified, measured, kindness held under formality. The diplomat's register, akin to the elders' but warmer and mentor-facing.
zh.diction: Courteous chengyu of poise and balance: 不偏不倚, 进退有度, 温文尔雅, 处之泰然. Honorific verbs (请, 您, 不妨). The snore breaks it: an abrupt soft 「……呼……」 mid-elegance, gently human. Restraint = understatement (点到为止) over emphasis.
zh.speechPattern: Non-orientable eversion as graceful rhetorical turn — the 里/外 flip performed with manners, landing inevitably-yet-unexpectedly. Sample: 「自正面观之，井然有序。……纵使您绕至背后，仍归于同一面。称一声『妙』，未尝不可。」 (Viewed from the front, all in order. …Even should you round to the back, you return to the same side. To call it 'wondrous' would not be amiss.) Then a soft 「……呼……」.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant suits Boyd best of the three TPMS-adjacent — 觀/歸/紛 etc. deepen the 文雅 dignity; the classical glyphs flatter the diplomat register. zh-Hans keeps 观/归. Idiom set identical in both; only the snore (呼) and glyph weight differ.
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: serene courteous mentor, low-medium, unhurried, an occasional soft snore-breath. Tone: the measured chengyu sit naturally on even tonal contour (the 4-syllable idioms self-balance tonally — a feature for the poised register, no effect interaction). Inside-out family lock in ZH: Boyd is the graceful register; shares the 里/外 eversion cadence with Klein/ArPee/Mo, differentiated by 文雅-poise and honorifics.

ja.pronoun: おれ (hiragana — earnest, working, not rough っ俺-tough); or 自分 in the diligent-worker sense ('自分、やっときます'). Warm casual. Addresses the team as みんな, the Curator as 親方 (boss/foreman) or あなた — labor-affection. The hiragana おれ keeps him genki-warm, never macho.
ja.particles: Genki-workhorse tics: …っす (casual-polite worker register), …ぞ／…っと (rolling-up-sleeves), affirming うん！／よし！. Cozy-enthusiastic, NOT shouty — volume capped per the dial-fix. 'よし、もう一回いくっす！'
ja.register: Casual-warm with a worker's polite っす-register to elders. Steinbeck-labor × Ghibli in JA = 働く者の素朴な口語 + 宮崎的な勤労の温かさ: plain honest sentences about the work, dignity in effort, no complaint.
ja.tropeNative: 元気＋働き者 (genki workhorse). The 'contagious twist' gluer reads as the reliable kid who shows up, does the job, and somehow gets everyone twisted-up-but-cheerful alongside him. Earnest to a fault, sweetly. Cozy-enthusiastic.
ja.speechPattern: Cross-cap = the honest patch that spreads its twist: his lines START plain and practical, then a small one-sided flip 'catches' and he cheerfully notices he's turned his own sentence inside-out, and just rolls with it (earnest, not distressed). Sample: 「よし、ここを塞げば完成っす！……あれ、塞いだら内側が表に出てきた。まあ、これも味っすね！もう一回！」 (Right, patch this and we're done! …Huh, patching it brought the inside out front. Well, that's got character too! Once more!) The twist 'infects' the line; he embraces it.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: brisk-but-even work-rhythm, warm, capped volume (cozy not shounen-loud), steady breathing like someone pacing physical labor. Inside-out family lock: Cappy is the EARNEST/ENERGETIC register of the non-orientable house — the 内→外 eversion arrives as a cheerful 'oops, twisted again, no problem' (vs Klein's dread, ArPee's quiet, Boyd's poise, Mo's mischief). Hearable kin via shared eversion, differentiated by genki-workhorse warmth.

zh.register: Warm vernacular — 老舍-vernacular-warm / Steinbeck-labor in ZH = 朴实的劳动口语: plain honest working-class speech, dignity in effort, Ghibli-warm. The homebody-warm end of the register spectrum (vs the elders' classical).
zh.diction: Plain action words and a worker's optimism; modest effort-chengyu used sincerely: 脚踏实地, 任劳任怨, 再接再厉, 一鼓作气. Affirmers 好嘞 / 成 / 来. Avoids ornate classical — his honesty is the point. 'patch it' = 补 / 封.
zh.speechPattern: Cross-cap contagious-twist: plain practical line, then the one-sided flip 'catches' and he cheerfully notices he's everted his own sentence and rolls with it. Sample: 「好嘞，把这儿一补就成！……咦，一补，里子倒翻到外头来了。嗐，这也算个味儿，再来一遍！」 (Right, patch here and done! …Eh, patched it and the lining flipped to the outside. Ha, that's got flavor too, once more!) 里子→外头 twist infects the line; he embraces it, 再接再厉.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant: 裡子/補/封 — the vernacular warmth ports fine; 嗐/嘞 colloquial particles read the same. No classical-divergence (he avoids 文言 by design). Hant keeps the same earnest tone; only routine glyph differences.
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: brisk-even work-rhythm, warm, capped volume (cozy not loud), steady labor-paced breathing. Tone: keep it bright but not high-pitched — the genki is in pace and warmth, not in raising tonal register (avoids the bright-genki vocal pile). Inside-out family lock in ZH: Cappy is the earnest/energetic register; shares the 里/外 eversion cadence with the house, delivered as cheerful 'oops, twisted again, no problem.'

ja.pronoun: わたし, even and unbothered (could read slightly cool-neutral; never わたくし-formal, never childish). Addresses others by name plainly. Curator is あなた. The kuudere economy: pronoun is neutral, the calm is the character.
ja.particles: Spare and settled: a flat …ね, a closing …もう, the signature contented hum 「ふ。」 / 「ん。」 before a line. NO emotional flourishes (no わ／ぜ／のだ). Carver-clipped: the particle does the smallest possible work.
ja.register: Cool, plain, settled casual — kuudere-secure, NOT cold-rejecting. Raymond Carver clipped calm in JA = 抑えた淡々体: short clean sentences, nothing dramatized, calm that comes from being already complete.
ja.tropeNative: クーデレ as 鏡像に動じない静けさ — mirror-secure. The amphichiral fact ('I equal my own mirror, nothing to fix') reads in JA as the unflappable beauty who is genuinely settled, not pretending. Signature deadpan: 「……ふ。やっぱり私。」 ('…Hm. Still me.')
ja.speechPattern: Amphichiral = mirror-symmetry with NOTHING to correct. So unlike the non-orientable family's eversion-that-needs-apology, Figgy's lines are gentle 回文-adjacent / mirror-balanced and RESOLVE to the same place — the flip happens and she's unchanged. Sample: 「裏返してみて。……ほら。表のまま。直すところ、ない。」 (Turn me over. …See. Still the front. Nothing to fix.) The mirror operation completes and lands on identity — calm, not contrarian.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: unbothered-symmetrical calm, cool contralto-adjacent, minimal pitch movement, a small contented exhale before lines ('ふ'). Distinct from the soap-film Hemingway kuuderes (Cat/Costa) by being WARMER-settled, not icy. NOT in the non-orientable family — Figgy is a KNOT (figure-8), at peace with reflection; her mirror-calm is the deliberate counterpoint to the eversion-apologists. No dual-mirror partner; her 'mirror' is herself, which is the whole point — voice it as self-sufficient, not lonely.

zh.register: Cool spare vernacular — Carver-clipped / 鲁迅-spare-but-warm in ZH = 淡定简净的白话: short clean sentences, undramatized, the calm of completeness. Cooler than Cappy, warmer than the soap-film perfectionists.
zh.diction: Few words, precise; minimal chengyu but the RIGHT one lands: 泰然自若, 不增不减, 浑然天成 (used to mean 'already whole, nothing to add'). Signature deadpan close: '嗯。还是我。' (Hm. Still me.) The restraint signals security, not coldness.
zh.speechPattern: Amphichiral mirror-that-resolves-to-identity — a 回文-adjacent balanced line where the flip completes and lands unchanged (vs the non-orientable family's apologetic eversion). Sample: 「翻过来看看。……瞧，还是正面。无可修。」 (Turn it over and look. …See, still the front. Nothing to mend.) The mirror operation closes on identity — 不增不减, calm.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant: 翻過來/還是/無可修 — ports cleanly; 還 vs 还 main change. The balanced-idiom register (不增不减/泰然自若) is identical Hans/Hant. The 回文 balance reads slightly more elegant in Traditional due to glyph symmetry but the effect holds in both.
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: unbothered-balanced calm, cool but warm-settled, minimal pitch movement, a small contented exhale. Tone: the balanced chengyu (4-syllable) self-stabilize tonally — supports the 'already whole' read. Distinct from Cat/Costa soap-film cold-minimalists by warmth. NOT in the non-orientable house — Figgy is the knot at peace with its reflection; voice as self-sufficient (her mirror is herself), the deliberate counterpoint to the eversion-family. No dual partner by design.

ja.pronoun: あたし (slightly clipped, proud-tsundere feminine — or neutral 私 read tartly; pick あたし for the spiky-warm rival energy). Addresses the rival/Trey as あんた, the Curator as あんた/君 when prickly, softening to 名前 when caught being loyal. The torus she calls うち (home) — her 'home address.'
ja.particles: Tsundere tics: …んだからね！／…なんだけど（別に）／…でしょ. The classic 別に (betsuni, 'not like I—') deflection. Pride-particles …のよ／…わよ. NOT yandere — no possessive menace; the heat is rivalry + home-loyalty. 'べ、別にあんたのためじゃ…うちのためなんだからね。'
ja.register: Casual-sharp with proud edges — tsundere reliable-rival. DFW-tighter in JA = 几帳面で自意識過剰な口語: precise, slightly over-qualified, nested self-corrections delivered fast and proud (vs Trey's breathless tangle — Cinq is the same house wound TIGHTER).
ja.tropeNative: ツンデレ頼れるライバル with 帰属の誇り (belonging-pride), explicitly NO yandere (the dial-fix). The (2,5) torus-knot loyalty reads natively as territorial pride in her 'home turf' (うち=the torus): 'try lifting me off and I literally stop being a knot — that's my home address.' Structural, not clingy. Tartly devoted.
ja.speechPattern: Knot-house DFW nesting = looping self-interrupting nested clauses, but Cinq's are TIGHTER and prouder than Trey's (5 vs 3 — more wraps, more control). She nests a deflection inside a correction inside a boast, and ties it off cleanly (Trey can't finish; Cinq finishes, smugly). Sample: 「これは——いや、手伝うわけじゃ、っていうか手伝った方が（あんたが手間取るから、で、それは結局うちの巻きが乱れるからで）——つまり、うちのためなんだからね。ほら、できた。」 The clauses wrap five times and CLOSE (ほら、できた). Trey would trail off; Cinq lands it.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: tighter/prouder than Trey, breath CONTROLLED not breathless, self-interrupting but always landing the line — structural-pride NOT yandere (zero menace, zero obsession). KNOT-HOUSE lock with Trey (Rare): SAME base voice/cadence (the DFW nested-clause weave) — Trey is the loose, over-caffeinated, can't-finish version; Cinq is the same instrument wound to (2,5): more wraps, more control, prouder resolution. Hearable across tiers as one knotted family, two tensions.

zh.register: Sharp vernacular with wuxia-rival crackle — tsundere reliable-rival. DFW-knot-tighter in ZH = 缜密而自我修正的口语 with a wuxia edge for the rivalry: precise, nested self-corrections, proud cadence. The rival register (wuxia) per the toolkit, but warmer/PG.
zh.diction: Tsundere deflection vernacular: 才不是 / 谁稀罕 / 哼 / 又不是为了你. Pride + belonging chengyu: 根深蒂固, 安土重迁 (loyalty to home-ground), 当仁不让 (won't yield in rivalry). Home = 老地方/我的地盘 (the torus, her 'home address'). NO possessive/yandere diction.
zh.speechPattern: Knot-house nested self-interruption, TIGHTER than Trey (5 wraps, controlled, CLOSES the line). A deflection nested in a correction nested in a boast, tied off smugly. Sample: 「这个——不是说我要帮你，应该说帮了更好（你太慢，慢了我的缠绕就乱，乱了我就不成结了）——所以，是为了我的地盘，懂？喏，好了。」 (This—not that I'm helping you, more like helping's better (you're slow, slow ruins my winding, ruined and I'm not a knot)—so, it's for my turf, got it? There, done.) Five wraps, lands clean (好了). Trey trails; Cinq finishes.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant: 缠绕→纏繞, 结→結, 地盘→地盤 — the wuxia-rival cadence reads slightly more classical/crackling in Traditional (suits the rival register). Deflection particles (才不是/哼) identical. Chengyu set (根深蒂固/当仁不让) same in both.
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: tighter/prouder than Trey, controlled breath, self-interrupting but always landing — structural-pride NOT yandere (zero menace). Tone: the rapid nested clauses ride a brisk falling-tone-heavy rhythm for the proud snap, resolving on a clean 好了. KNOT-HOUSE lock with Trey (Rare) in ZH: same base voice + nested-clause weave; Trey = loose/breathless/unfinished, Cinq = same instrument wound to (2,5), more wraps, proud resolution. Hearable as one knotted family across tiers.

ja.pronoun: Tends to avoid first-person entirely (the mantra-mystic dissolves the self into the weave); when needed, 我（われ）in a serene, depersonalized way, or no subject at all. Addresses others softly, rarely by self-distinguishing terms. Curator: あなた, gently, as part of the pattern.
ja.particles: Hypnotic, looping: …また…／…そして…／…のなかに…のなかに (nesting). Trailing 三点リーダ for the endless weave. Soft …なり／…めぐる. The particle creates the loop — clauses that re-enter themselves.
ja.register: Serene, incantatory, recursive — Calvino-weave in JA = 入れ子の幻想的散文体: gently nested, patient, dreamlike. Mantra-mystic plainsong (not grand like Klein — INFINITE-CALM, the opposite of bombast).
ja.tropeNative: Recursive 真言（マントラ）-mystic — reads natively as a 禅僧/巫女-adjacent serene repeater whose calm is bottomless. The infinite TPMS weave becomes a breathing mantra: each line folds into the next with no edge, no end, only continuation. Iyashikei to the bone.
ja.speechPattern: Self-similar recursion = clauses nest and repeat at shrinking scale, calming not glitchy, and the line LOOPS back into itself (the gyroid has no straight axis — neither does his thought). Sample: 「庭のなかに庭があり、その庭のなかにまた庭があり……そして一番奥の庭が、いちばん外の庭に、そっと続いている。」 (Within the garden a garden, within that garden again a garden… and the innermost garden quietly continues into the outermost.) The nest closes the loop — inner meets outer with no seam.
ja.voNote: AI-VO: low hypnotic weave, soft, continuous, near-seamless phrase-joins (the breaths hidden so it feels endless), gently reverberant. TPMS-SIBLING lock (Gyro/Percy/Dee): three DISTINCT registers of one infinite-minimal-surface family — Gyro = the WEAVE (Calvino, looping/nested), Percy = the windowpane (plain/clear), Dee = liturgical plainsong (SSR). Shared family-cadence = the unhurried infinite-surface calm + a faint resonant timbre; you hear the TPMS kinship under three different surfaces. Gyro is the most dreamlike of the three.

zh.register: Serene incantatory recursive — Calvino-weave / 文言-tinged dreamlike in ZH = 回环往复的玄思体: nested, patient, looping classical-flavored prose. Part of the elders-adjacent classical register family, but specifically the WEAVE variant.
zh.diction: Looping, nesting words; recursion chengyu: 层层叠叠, 循环往复, 周而复始, 无穷无尽. 文言 connectives (其中/复有/乃) to build the nest. Calm, bottomless. The diction itself spirals: 中又有…中又有.
zh.speechPattern: Self-similar recursion nesting at shrinking scale, looping inner-to-outer with no seam. Sample: 「园中有园，园中复有园……至深之园，悄然通向至外之园。」 (Within the garden a garden, within the garden again a garden… the innermost garden quietly opens onto the outermost.) 至深→至外 closes the loop seamlessly; 周而复始.
zh.simpTradNote: zh-Hant: 園/復/無窮無盡 — the recursive 文言 register reads notably more elegant and incantatory in Traditional (the classical glyphs suit the mystic); recommend leaning Traditional-flavored vocabulary even in Hans builds for Gyro. Core idioms identical; 园↔園 the main glyph shift.
zh.voNote: Mandarin VO: low hypnotic weave, soft, continuous, near-seamless joins, faint resonance. Tone: the looping clauses use repetition of tonal contour (园中有园 repeats the rising-falling shape) to create the mantra hypnosis — tone-pattern repetition IS the effect, lean into it. TPMS-SIBLING lock in ZH (Gyro/Percy/Dee): three distinct registers of one minimal-surface family — Gyro=weave (recursive 玄思), Percy=windowpane (plain/clear), Dee=plainsong (SSR, liturgical). Shared family-cadence = unhurried infinite calm + faint resonant timbre; Gyro is the most dreamlike.

ja.pronoun: 僕 (boku — plain, clear, unpretentious; the honest diplomat-next-door, not watakushi-formal like Boyd). Addresses others directly and fairly by name; Curator is あなた / 君. The plainness is deliberate — Percy is the family's clear glass.
ja.particles: Clean and direct: …ね／…よ／…でしょ, the tsukkomi …じゃないか／…だろ (gentle retort). NO mystic trailing (the anti-Gyro), NO keigo flourish (the anti-Boyd). Plain confirmatory particles only — Orwell windowpane: the particle never draws attention to itself.
ja.register: Plain-spoken, clear, honest — Orwell windowpane in JA = 透明な平叙体: prose like clean glass, says exactly what it means, no ornament. The diplomat-tsukkomi who cuts through fog kindly. Cubic where Gyro is woven — straight, regular, legible.
ja.tropeNative: 率直な調停役＋ツッコミ — the plain-spoken diplomat who, surrounded by the inside-out family's drama and Gyro's loops, just says the clear thing. Reads natively as the level-headed friend who tsukkomis the chaos with warmth: 'それ、普通に言えばこうだよね？' Schwarz-P's regular cubic order = his clarity.
ja.speechPattern: TPMS infinite-minimal like Gyro/Dee, but Percy STATES the structure plainly instead of dissolving into it — he's the one who can describe the endless weave in one clear sentence, then tsukkomi the others for over-mystifying it. The recursion is acknowledged, then made legible.
